title: Module 2 Project: Warehouse Stock Ledger Schema
summary: Create warehouses and stock_movements through six cumulative schema steps.

sketchBlocks.title: Understanding the Warehouse Schema
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown: In this project, you will create a schema for a warehouse stock ledger. This schema will help manage stock movements and warehouse details efficiently. The schema will consist of two main tables: `warehouses` and `stock_movements`. Each table will have specific constraints to ensure data integrity and enforce business rules. By the end of this project, you will have a robust schema that can handle stock receipts and adjustments while maintaining data consistency.

sketchBlocks.title: Defining Tables and Constraints
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown: The `warehouses` table will store information about each warehouse, including a unique identifier and a name. It will also include a status column with a default value to indicate whether a warehouse is active. The `stock_movements` table will track changes in stock levels, linking each movement to a specific warehouse. This table will include constraints to ensure that all necessary data is provided and that each movement is associated with a valid warehouse. By using primary keys, foreign keys, and other constraints, you will create a schema that supports reliable data management.

quizDraft.title: Create the Warehouse Table
quizDraft.prompt: Create a `warehouses` table with `id` as INTEGER and `name` as TEXT. Verify the table creation using a query on `sqlite_master`.
quizDraft.hint: Define the table with two columns: `id` and `name`.

help.concept: Creating a basic table with specific columns.
help.hint_1: Use CREATE TABLE to define the table structure.
help.hint_2: Ensure the table has columns for `id` and `name`.

quizDraft.starterCode:
-- Verify the table creation
SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='warehouses';
quizDraft.solutionCode:
-- Verify the table creation
SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='warehouses';

quizDraft.title: Add Identity and Name Rules
quizDraft.prompt: Modify the `warehouses` table to make `id` a PRIMARY KEY and `name` NOT NULL and UNIQUE. Verify the changes using a query on `sqlite_master`.
quizDraft.hint: Use PRIMARY KEY for `id` and constraints for `name`.

help.concept: Enhancing table constraints for identity and uniqueness.
help.hint_1: Modify the table to include PRIMARY KEY and NOT NULL constraints.
help.hint_2: Ensure `name` is UNIQUE to prevent duplicates.

quizDraft.title: Add an Operational Default
quizDraft.prompt: Add a `status` column to the `warehouses` table with a default value of 'active'. Verify the addition using a query on `sqlite_master`.
quizDraft.hint: Include a DEFAULT constraint for the `status` column.

help.concept: Adding a column with a default value to a table.
help.hint_1: Use DEFAULT to set a default value for `status`.
help.hint_2: Ensure the `status` column is NOT NULL with a default.

quizDraft.title: Create the Movement Table
quizDraft.prompt: Create a `stock_movements` table with columns for `id`, `warehouse_id`, `sku`, `quantity_change`, `movement_type`, and `note`. Verify the table creation using a query on `sqlite_master`.
quizDraft.hint: Define the table with all required columns.

help.concept: Creating a new table with multiple columns.
help.hint_2: Ensure each column is defined with an appropriate data type.

quizDraft.starterCode:
-- Verify the table creation
SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='stock_movements';
quizDraft.solutionCode:
-- Verify the table creation
SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='stock_movements';

starterFiles.content:
-- Define the stock_movements table
CREATE TABLE stock_movements (
    id INTEGER,
    warehouse_id INTEGER,
    sku TEXT,
    quantity_change REAL,
    movement_type TEXT,
    note TEXT
);

-- Verify the table creation
SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='stock_movements';

solutionFiles.content:
-- Define the stock_movements table
CREATE TABLE stock_movements (
    id INTEGER,
    warehouse_id INTEGER,
    sku TEXT,
    quantity_change REAL,
    movement_type TEXT,
    note TEXT
);

-- Verify the table creation

quizDraft.checkSql: SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='stock_movements';

quizDraft.title: Protect Required Movement Data
quizDraft.prompt: Modify the `stock_movements` table to make `id` a PRIMARY KEY and require `warehouse_id`, `sku`, `quantity_change`, and `movement_type` while allowing `note` to remain NULL. Verify the changes using a query on `sqlite_master`.
quizDraft.hint: Use PRIMARY KEY for `id` and NOT NULL for required columns.

help.concept: Enhancing table constraints for required data.
help.hint_1: Ensure `id` is a PRIMARY KEY and other columns are NOT NULL except `note`.
help.hint_2: Verify the constraints using a query on `sqlite_master`.

quizDraft.starterCode:
-- Verify the table constraints
SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='stock_movements';
quizDraft.solutionCode:
-- Verify the table constraints
SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='stock_movements';

starterFiles.content:
-- Update the stock_movements table with constraints
CREATE TABLE stock_movements (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    warehouse_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    sku TEXT NOT NULL,
    quantity_change REAL NOT NULL,
    movement_type TEXT NOT NULL,
    note TEXT
);

-- Verify the table constraints
SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='stock_movements';

solutionFiles.content:
-- Update the stock_movements table with constraints
CREATE TABLE stock_movements (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    warehouse_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    sku TEXT NOT NULL,
    quantity_change REAL NOT NULL,
    movement_type TEXT NOT NULL,
    note TEXT
);

-- Verify the table constraints
SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='stock_movements';

quizDraft.checkSql: SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='stock_movements';

quizDraft.title: Connect Movements to Warehouses
quizDraft.prompt: Add a FOREIGN KEY constraint to `stock_movements` linking `warehouse_id` to `warehouses(id)`. Set a default value for `movement_type`. Verify the changes using a query on `sqlite_master`.
quizDraft.hint: Use FOREIGN KEY to link `warehouse_id` to `warehouses`.

help.concept: Establishing a foreign key relationship between tables.
help.hint_1: Add a FOREIGN KEY constraint to `warehouse_id`.
help.hint_2: Set a default value for `movement_type` and verify the schema.

quizDraft.starterCode:
-- Verify the foreign key constraint
SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='stock_movements';
quizDraft.solutionCode:
-- Verify the foreign key constraint
SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='stock_movements';

starterFiles.content:
-- Update the stock_movements table with a foreign key
CREATE TABLE stock_movements (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    warehouse_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    sku TEXT NOT NULL,
    quantity_change REAL NOT NULL,
    movement_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'receipt',
    note TEXT,
    FOREIGN KEY (warehouse_id) REFERENCES warehouses(id)
);

-- Verify the foreign key constraint
SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='stock_movements';

solutionFiles.content:
-- Update the stock_movements table with a foreign key
CREATE TABLE stock_movements (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    warehouse_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    sku TEXT NOT NULL,
    quantity_change REAL NOT NULL,
    movement_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'receipt',
    note TEXT,
    FOREIGN KEY (warehouse_id) REFERENCES warehouses(id)
);

-- Verify the foreign key constraint
SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='stock_movements';

quizDraft.checkSql: SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='stock_movements';
